fields.name: Beginner 1
fields.description: First contact with Arabic. Letters, sounds, greetings, numbers.

fields.name: Beginner 2
fields.description: Familiar daily expressions and basic phrases.

fields.name: Intermediate 1
fields.description: Can deal with most situations encountered while travelling in an Arabic-speaking area.

fields.name: Intermediate 2
fields.description: Can interact with a degree of fluency and spontaneity. Reads articles and reports.

fields.name: Advanced 1
fields.description: Can express ideas fluently. Uses language flexibly for social, academic and professional purposes.

fields.name: Advanced 2
fields.description: Near-native command. Can summarise complex spoken or written sources.

fields.name: Grammar
fields.description: Sentence structure, verb forms, particles.

fields.name: Vocabulary
fields.description: Build your word bank step by step.

fields.name: Reading
fields.description: Comprehension and authentic texts.

fields.name: Listening
fields.description: Audio comprehension and dictation.

fields.name: Writing
fields.description: Producing written Arabic.

fields.name: Conversation
fields.description: Speaking practice and dialogue.

fields.title: Arabic Alphabet & Sounds
fields.description: Master all 28 Arabic letters and their sounds. Letters in isolation, in words, and the rules that change their shape.

fields.title: First 100 Arabic Words
fields.description: The most useful 100 words in everyday Arabic. Pictures, audio, and a memory game in every lesson.

fields.title: Greetings & Introductions
fields.description: How to say hello, ask someone's name, and exchange basic information in Arabic.

fields.title: Reading Simple Texts
fields.description: Three short Arabic readings with vocabulary and comprehension questions.

fields.title: Numbers & Time
fields.description: From counting to telling time and giving dates in Arabic.

fields.title: Arabic for Kids: Animals & Colors
fields.description: Bright pictures, songs, and games. Learn 30 animals and 10 colors in Arabic.

fields.title: Listening: News & Stories
fields.description: Train your ear with short news clips and recorded stories. Slow versions and full-speed versions.

fields.title: Teens: Daily Life Conversations
fields.description: Real-life dialogues for teens: school, friends, social media, family discussions.

fields.title: Advanced Grammar: Conditionals
fields.description: إذا، لو، إن — the three conditionals in Arabic, what makes them different, and when to use each.

fields.title: Pronunciation Workshop
fields.description: A no-prerequisite course on the trickiest Arabic sounds for non-native speakers: ع، غ، خ، ح، ق، ض.

fields.title: Learn Arabic from scratch  The Speaking Course for Absolute Beginners
fields.description:
A 7-video 
Learn Arabic from scratch, The Speaking Course for Absolute Beginners. Watch each video, mark the lesson complete when you're ready to move on. (Rename the course and lesson titles in /admin/ to match the actual playlist content.)

fields.title: Arabic for Kids — My First Steps
fields.description:
A complete sixteen-lesson introduction to Arabic for young learners. Starts with the alphabet, teaches the rules that turn letters into sounds (positions, harakat, sun and moon letters), then moves into everyday vocabulary.

By the end your child can READ Arabic — not just recognise letters — and can greet, introduce themselves, count to ten, and name colors, family, animals, and the days of the week. Built for ages 6–12. Each lesson runs 8–13 minutes.

fields.title: Daily Life Skills for Kids
fields.description: Help children learn independence, responsibility, and daily habits.

fields.title: Welcome & how this course works
fields.body_markdown: Welcome to Arabic Alphabet & Sounds. Six chapters, twenty-eight letters, all the trickiest sounds.

fields.title: The first six letters: ا ب ت ث ج ح
fields.body_markdown: These six letters are the foundation. Pay attention to dot placement.

fields.title: Letters that connect & letters that don't
fields.body_markdown: Six letters in Arabic only connect from the right, never to the left: ا د ذ ر ز و.

fields.title: Short vowels (harakat)
fields.body_markdown: Fatha, Kasra, Damma — three little marks that change everything.

fields.title: People & family (15 words)
fields.body_markdown: أب (father), أم (mother), أخ (brother), أخت (sister)…

fields.title: House & home (20 words)
fields.body_markdown: بيت (house), باب (door), غرفة (room), مطبخ (kitchen)…

fields.title: Food & drink (25 words)
fields.body_markdown: خبز (bread), ماء (water), شاي (tea), قهوة (coffee)…

fields.title: Numbers 1–20
fields.body_markdown: واحد، اثنان، ثلاثة، أربعة، خمسة…

fields.title: Days of the week & months
fields.body_markdown: الأحد، الاثنين، الثلاثاء…

fields.title: Saying hello
fields.body_markdown: مرحبا (Hello), السلام عليكم (Peace be upon you), أهلا (Welcome)…

fields.title: "What's your name?"
fields.body_markdown: ما اسمك؟ — اسمي [your name]

fields.title: "Where are you from?"
fields.body_markdown: من أين أنت؟ — أنا من [country]

fields.title: Saying goodbye
fields.body_markdown: مع السلامة (Goodbye), إلى اللقاء (See you later)

fields.title: My day
fields.body_markdown: في الصباح، أستيقظ في الساعة السابعة…

fields.title: At the market
fields.body_markdown: ذهبت إلى السوق لشراء الفاكهة والخضار…

fields.title: A letter from my friend
fields.body_markdown: عزيزي أحمد، كيف حالك؟ أنا بخير…

fields.title: Numbers 21–100
fields.body_markdown: واحد وعشرون، اثنان وعشرون…

fields.title: Telling the time
fields.body_markdown: كم الساعة؟ — الساعة الثالثة والنصف.

fields.title: Days, months, and years
fields.body_markdown: يناير، فبراير، مارس…

fields.title: Talking about your schedule
fields.body_markdown: يوم الإثنين عندي عمل في الساعة التاسعة.

fields.title: Pets at home
fields.body_markdown: قطة (cat), كلب (dog), طائر (bird), سمكة (fish)

fields.title: Farm animals
fields.body_markdown: بقرة (cow), خروف (sheep), دجاجة (chicken), حصان (horse)

fields.title: Wild animals
fields.body_markdown: أسد (lion), فيل (elephant), زرافة (giraffe), قرد (monkey)

fields.title: Colors of the rainbow
fields.body_markdown: أحمر، برتقالي، أصفر، أخضر، أزرق، نيلي، بنفسجي

fields.title: A weather report
fields.body_markdown: الطقس اليوم مشمس مع رياح خفيفة…

fields.title: Headlines from the morning news
fields.body_markdown: هذه أبرز عناوين النشرة الصباحية…

fields.title: A short folk tale
fields.body_markdown: كان يا ما كان في قديم الزمان…

fields.title: At school
fields.body_markdown: كيف كان الامتحان؟ — كان صعبا جدا!

fields.title: Talking about social media
fields.body_markdown: هل رأيت الفيديو الجديد؟

fields.title: Plans with friends
fields.body_markdown: متى نلتقي يوم الجمعة؟

fields.title: Real conditionals with إذا
fields.body_markdown: إذا followed by past tense expresses real, likely conditions.

fields.title: Hypothetical conditionals with لو
fields.body_markdown: لو is for unlikely or impossible conditions, often in the past.

fields.title: Negative conditionals with إن لم
fields.body_markdown: إن لم: "if not". Followed by jussive (الجزم).

fields.title: The "ع" sound
fields.body_markdown: ع is voiced and pharyngeal. We'll show you exactly where to feel it.

fields.title: The "ح" sound
fields.body_markdown: ح is voiceless and pharyngeal — much "deeper" than English h.

fields.title: "خ" vs "غ"
fields.body_markdown: Two sounds that beginners confuse — and how to tell them apart.

fields.title: Emphatic letters: ص ض ط ظ
fields.body_markdown: The four emphatic consonants and how they color surrounding vowels.

fields.title: Sun and Moon letters (الـ)
fields.body_markdown:
The word for "the" is الـ (al-). But sometimes the "L" is silent.

- **Moon Letters**: Say the "L". (e.g., Al-Qamar / The Moon)
- **Sun Letters**: Skip the "L" and double the next letter. (e.g., Ash-Shams / The Sun)

- **Sun Letters**: Skip the "L" and double the next letter. (e.g., Ash-Shams / The Sun)

fields.title: Cleaning Your Room
fields.body_markdown:
تنظيف الغرفة
           الحفاظ على نظافة غرفتك أمر مهم. ابدأ بجمع ألعابك ووضعها في مكانها الصحيح. ثم قم بطي ملابسك ووضعها بشكل مرتب. الغرفة النظيفة
 تساعدك على الشعور بالراحة والتنظيم.

fields.title: Washing Hands Properly
fields.body_markdown:
غسل اليدين بشكل صحيح
      غسل اليدين يساعد في الحفاظ على صحتك. استخدم الصابون والماء وافرك يديك لمدة 20 ثانية على الأقل ثم اشطفهما جيدًا.

fields.title: Getting Ready for School
fields.body_markdown:
الاستعداد للمدرسة
 الاستعداد للمدرسة هو جزء مهم من الروتين اليومي. جهّز حقيبتك في الليلة السابقة، حضّر ملابسك، واستيقظ في الوقت المناسب. هذا يساعدك على بدء يومك بدون توتر.

fields.title: Healthy Eating
fields.body_markdown:
الأكل الصحي
تناول الطعام الصحي يساعد جسمك على النمو بقوة. حاول تناول الفواكه والخضروات و**شرب الماء**. تجنب تناول الكثير من السكر والوجبات غير الصحية. الأكل الصحي يمنحك الطاقة للتعلم واللعب.

fields.text: Which of these letters has TWO dots?
fields.explanation: ت has two dots above; ث has three.

fields.text: Which letter has a dot BELOW it?
How do you say "brother" in Arabic?
How do you say "It's 3:30" in Arabic?
Which conditional is for unreal or hypothetical situations?
fields.explanation: لو is used for unreal conditions.

fields.text: How many letters are in the Arabic alphabet?
Which letters NEVER connect to the next letter? (Select all that apply.)
fields.explanation: ا د ذ ر ز و — six "non-connectors".

fields.text: A1 — How do you say "Hello" in Arabic?
fields.explanation: مرحبا is the most common informal greeting.

fields.text: A2 — Pick the correct article: ___ كتاب  (the book)
B1 — Which is a sound masculine plural?
fields.explanation: مدرسون is a sound masculine plural.

fields.text: B1 — Which of these are personal pronouns? (Select all that apply.)
B2 — Verb form II (فعّل) typically expresses...
fields.explanation: Form II commonly adds intensity or causation.

fields.title: Write a paragraph about how you clean your room
fields.description: Write 3–5 simple sentences about how you clean your room using easy words.
fields.instructions:
### 🧹 Assignment: My Clean Room

Write a short paragraph about how you clean your room.

### ✍️ Instructions:
- Write 3 to 5 sentences
- Use simple words and sentences
- Start your sentences with "I"
- You can write in English or Arabic

fields.text_response:
English:
I clean my room every day. I pick up my toys and put them in the box. I make my bed and fold my clothes. I sweep the floor and throw away trash.

Arabic:
أنا أنظف غرفتي كل يوم. أرتب ألعابي وأضعها في الصندوق. أرتب سريري وأطوي ملابسي. أنظف الأرض وأرمي القمامة.

fields.english_sentence: I go to school
This is a new book
The house is big and beautiful
I love reading
The sun rises in the morning
The water is cold and delicious
The boy plays in the garden
The cat sleeps on the sofa
I drink water
The school is near the house
The child eats the apple
I write with the pen
A book is a reading tool
The dog flies in the sky
The sun lights the day
Water eats food
A house is a place to live
The moon appears at night
The airplane swims in the sea
The teacher teaches students
A pen is used for writing
The cat barks like a dog
The car drives on the road
An apple is a type of vegetable
